title: Vividly Peppery Slovakian Black Pudding Papillote
description: A traditional Slovakian dish featuring black pudding, this recipe is perfect for a sick day, with a dairy-free friendly twist and a peppery kick
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, combine the sliced onion, minced garlic, smoked paprika, black pepper, and salt, and mix until the onion is evenly coated.
Add the sliced black pudding to the bowl and mix gently until the pudding is evenly distributed among the onion mixture.
Brush one side of each parchment paper square with olive oil.
Place a quarter of the black pudding and onion mixture onto the oiled side of each parchment paper square, leaving a 1 inch border around the edges.
Fold the parchment paper over the filling to form a triangle or square shape, and press the edges together to seal the papillote.
Place the papillotes onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, leaving about 1 inch of space between each papillote.
Bake the papillotes in the preheated oven for 25 minutes, or until the parchment paper is puffed and golden brown.
Remove the papillotes from the oven and let them cool for a few minutes before serving.
Serve the papillotes hot, garnished with fresh parsley or chives if desired.
